---
title: "impl_calvin"
output: html_document
---


## K Nearest Neighbors

```{r}
library(tidymodels)
library(yardstick)
library(gridExtra)
library(patchwork)

# load preprocessed data
train = read.csv('train.csv')
test = read.csv('test.csv')

# convert target column to factor
train$price_tier <- as.factor(train$price_tier)
test$price_tier <- as.factor(test$price_tier)

# standardization: important for knn as knn is sensitive to scale
rec <- recipe(price_tier ~ ., data = train) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())


# ================================================
#               HYPERPARAMETER TUNING
# ================================================

# Define model and workflow
knn_def <- nearest_neighbor(
  mode = 'classification',
  neighbors  = tune(),
  dist_power  = tune(),
  weight_func = "rectangular" # unweighted -> standard kNN
) %>% 
  set_engine("kknn")

knn_wf <- workflow() %>%
  add_model(knn_def) %>%
  add_recipe(rec)

# Cross-validation folds
set.seed(5003)
cv_folds <- vfold_cv(train, v = 5, strata = price_tier)

# Define weighted metrics for multiclass classification
f_meas_weighted <- metric_tweak(
  .name = "f_meas_weighted",
  .fn   = yardstick::f_meas,
  estimator = "macro_weighted"
)

precision_weighted <- metric_tweak(
  .name = "precision_weighted",
  .fn   = yardstick::precision,
  estimator = "macro_weighted"
)

recall_weighted <- metric_tweak(
  .name = "recall_weighted",
  .fn   = yardstick::recall,
  estimator = "macro_weighted"
)

multiclass_metrics <- metric_set(
  yardstick::accuracy,
  yardstick::sens,
  yardstick::spec,
  yardstick::precision,
  yardstick::f_meas,
  f_meas_weighted,
  precision_weighted,
  recall_weighted
)

# Define parameter grid
param_grid <- tidyr::crossing(
  neighbors  = seq(5, 50, 5),
  dist_power = c(1, 2)
)

ctrl <- control_grid(save_pred = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)

# ------------------------------------------------
#        Load or perform tuning
# ------------------------------------------------

if (file.exists("knn_tune_res.rds")) {
  #cat("✅ Loading saved KNN tuning results...\n")
  knn_tune_res <- readRDS("knn_tune_res.rds")
} else {
  #cat("⚙️ Running KNN hyperparameter tuning (this may take a few minutes)...\n")
  
  knn_tune_res <- tune_grid(
    knn_wf,
    resamples = cv_folds,
    grid      = param_grid,
    metrics   = multiclass_metrics,
    control   = ctrl
  )
  
  saveRDS(knn_tune_res, file = "knn_tune_res.rds")
  #cat("💾 Tuning results saved to knn_tune_res.rds\n")
}




# ================================================
#                PLOT TUNING RESULTS
# ================================================

m <- collect_metrics(knn_tune_res)

if ("dist_power" %in% names(m)) {
  m <- m %>%
    mutate(dist_power = factor(dist_power,
                               levels = c(1, 2),
                               labels = c("L1 (p=1)", "L2 (p=2)")))
}

res_plot <- m %>%
  filter(.metric %in% c('f_meas_weighted')) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = neighbors, y = mean,
             color = if ('dist_power' %in% names(m)) dist_power else NULL)) +
  facet_wrap(~ .metric, scales = 'free_y') +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean - std_err, ymax = mean + std_err), width = 0.6) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(m$neighbors))) +
  labs(
    title = 'knn: CV Weighted F1-Score',
    x = 'k (neighbors)',
    y = 'CV mean \u00B1 SE',
    color = 'Distance'
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# ================================================
#     FINAL MODEL (HIGHEST WEIGHTED F1 SCORE)
# ================================================

if (file.exists("best_knn.rds")) {
  #cat("✅ Loading saved final KNN model...\n")
  best_knn <- readRDS("best_knn.rds")
} else {
  #cat("⚙️ Training final KNN model...\n")
  best_params <- select_best(knn_tune_res, metric = 'f_meas_weighted')
  best_knn_wf <- finalize_workflow(knn_wf, best_params)
  best_knn <- fit(best_knn_wf, data = train)
  saveRDS(best_knn, file = "best_knn.rds")
  #cat("💾 Final KNN model saved to best_knn.rds\n")
}



# ================================================
#     PLOTS TO EVALUATE FINAL MODEL PERFORMANCE
# ================================================

# Check if saved predictions exist
if (file.exists("best_knn_pred.rds")) {
  #message("Loading saved predictions...")
  best_knn_pred <- readRDS("best_knn_pred.rds")
} else {
  #message("Generating predictions and saving results...")
  best_knn_pred <- predict(best_knn, test) %>%
    bind_cols(test)
  saveRDS(best_knn_pred, file = "best_knn_pred.rds")
}

# Generate confusion matrix
cm <- conf_mat(best_knn_pred, truth = price_tier, estimate = .pred_class)

# Convert confusion matrix to a tidy data frame
cm_df <- as.data.frame(cm$table)
colnames(cm_df) <- c("Predicted", "Actual", "Freq")


# plot confusion matrix
cm_plot <- ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = Actual, y = Predicted, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", , linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), size = 3.5, color = "black", fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "#1E90FF") +
  labs(
    title = "Confusion Matrix",
    x = "Actual Price Tier",
    y = "Predicted Price Tier",
    fill = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +  # Reduced base size for grid layout
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 9),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 8),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 8),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 7)
  )



# classification report table
overall_acc <- sum(cm_df$Freq[cm_df$Predicted == cm_df$Actual]) / sum(cm_df$Freq)
class_levels <- levels(test$price_tier)

report_table <- map_dfr(class_levels, function(cls) {
  TP <- cm_df$Freq[cm_df$Actual == cls & cm_df$Predicted == cls]
  FN <- sum(cm_df$Freq[cm_df$Actual == cls & cm_df$Predicted != cls])
  FP <- sum(cm_df$Freq[cm_df$Actual != cls & cm_df$Predicted == cls])
  Support <- sum(cm_df$Freq[cm_df$Actual == cls])

  Precision <- TP / (TP + FP)
  Recall    <- TP / (TP + FN)
  F1        <- 2 * (Precision * Recall) / (Precision + Recall)

  tibble(
    Class = cls,
    Precision = Precision,
    Recall = Recall,
    F1 = F1,
    Support = Support
  )
})

macro_row <- report_table %>%
  summarise(
    Class = "Macro Avg",
    Precision = mean(Precision),
    Recall    = mean(Recall),
    F1        = mean(F1),
    Support   = sum(Support)
  )

weighted_row <- report_table %>%
  summarise(
    Class = "Weighted Avg",
    Precision = weighted.mean(Precision, w = Support),
    Recall    = weighted.mean(Recall, w = Support),
    F1        = weighted.mean(F1, w = Support),
    Support   = sum(Support)
  )

# save for result table
wp_knn <- weighted.mean(report_table$Precision, w = report_table$Support)
wr_knn <- weighted.mean(report_table$Recall, w = report_table$Support)
wf_knn <- weighted.mean(report_table$F1, w = report_table$Support)

# merge result into a single table
final_report <- bind_rows(report_table, macro_row, weighted_row) %>%
  mutate(across(c(Precision, Recall, F1), ~ round(.x, 4)))

display_df <- final_report %>%
  mutate(
    Precision = sprintf("%.4f", Precision),
    Recall    = sprintf("%.4f", Recall),
    F1        = sprintf("%.4f", F1)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Class, Precision, Recall, F1, Support)


row_n  <- nrow(display_df)
row_bg <- c(rep(c("#F8F9FA", "#FFFFFF"), length.out = max(row_n - 2, 0)),
            rep("#E8F4F8", min(2, row_n)))   # last two rows shaded

table_theme <- ttheme_minimal(
  base_size = 10,
  core = list(
    fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5),
    bg_params = list(fill = row_bg)
  ),
  colhead = list(
    fg_params = list(fontface = "bold", fontsize = 11, col = "white"),
    bg_params = list(fill = "#1E90FF")
  ),
  rowhead = list(fg_params = list(hjust = 0.5, x = 0.5))
)


tbl_grob <- tableGrob(display_df, rows = NULL, theme = table_theme)
tbl_grob$widths <- unit(rep(1, ncol(display_df)), "null")


classification_report_plot <- ggplot() +
  annotation_custom(
    grob = tbl_grob,
    xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf, ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title    = "Classification Report",
    subtitle = paste("Overall Accuracy:", sprintf("%.4f", overall_acc))
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 5)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)),
    plot.margin   = margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )



# ================================================
#                COMBINE VISUALIZATIONS
# ================================================
(res_plot | cm_plot) / classification_report_plot +
  plot_layout(heights = c(1, 2), guides = "collect")


```

The K-Nearest Neighbors model was optimized using cross-validation on weighted F1-score. The tuning process evaluated different neighborhood sizes (k) and distance metrics (L1 and L2). The results show that performance improves as k increases and stabilizes around k ≥ 25, with L1 distance consistently outperforming L2. The best-performing configuration selected by cross-validation is k = 45 and L1 distance. The final model achieved **71.1% overall accuracy**, with Budget predicted most reliably, Premium showing high precision but lower recall, and Standard being the most difficult class due to frequent confusion with Budget and Premium.
